macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US-Iran Hostilities Drive Oil to Weekly Surge and Global Stock Selloff
items.summary: Oil prices are set for their sharpest weekly rise in three months, with Brent crude near $85 a barrel, as renewed attacks between the U.S. and Iran limit flows through the Strait of Hormuz; this triggered a global equity selloff, with Asian stocks falling and U.S. equity futures extending losses.

items.title: Fed Officials Shift Hawkish: Dallas Fed President Logan Calls for Rate Hike
items.summary: Dallas Federal Reserve President Lorie Logan became the first of Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's new colleagues to publicly call for an interest-rate hike, while Vice Chair Philip Jefferson stated he would be open to raising rates if inflation does not improve near-term, despite softer June inflation data.

items.title: Tech Selloff and AI Jitters Tank Nasdaq; Oracle and Samsung Drop Sharply
items.summary: The Nasdaq Composite fell on Thursday due to a shakeout in U.S. semiconductor stocks and spreading AI jitters, with Oracle Corporation down 6.25% and Samsung Electronics plunging 8.77%, while TSMC reported a 77% profit jump beating estimates.

items.title: Major Index Movements: S&P 500 and Dow Jones Slide on Geopolitical and Tech Pressure
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 0.60% to 7,488.90 and the Dow Jones dropped 0.20% to 52,552.97, weighed down by chip stock declines, Alphabet shares, and Netflix guidance that disappointed investors.

items.title: Dollar Set for Weekly Drop Despite Strong Economic Data; Rupee Weakens on Oil
items.summary: The dollar is poised for a weekly decline as traders cut bets on imminent Fed rate hikes following softer inflation data, though it moved higher Thursday on strong economic reports; meanwhile, the Indian rupee weakened 17 paise to 96.42 against the dollar amid rising oil prices.

items.title: US Imposes New 25% Tariffs on Brazilian Imports Amid Trade Tensions
items.summary: The U.S. announced new 25% tariffs on some Brazilian imports over alleged unfair practices, though beef, coffee, and rare-earth materials were exempted, adding to global trade friction alongside Section 232 steel tariffs.

items.title: Inflation Slowed to 3.5% in June; China's Q2 Growth Hits Weakest Pace Since 2022
items.summary: U.S. inflation eased to 3.5% in June due to lower gasoline prices, while China's economy grew 4.3% in the second quarter, the weakest pace since 2022 despite strong exports and a $1.2 trillion trade surplus.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Retreats to $64,000 Amid Iran-U.S. Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin pulled back from a monthly high of $65,500 to trade near $64,174 as sell pressure intensified following U.S. airstrikes on Iranian power infrastructure and rising Gulf tensions, with Ethereum also declining 1.7%.

items.title: Ostium DeFi Protocol Exploited for $18–24M in Oracle Manipulation Attack
items.summary: BNB Chain’s Ostium suspended trading after hackers drained $18–24 million from its OLP vault via oracle manipulation, highlighting ongoing DeFi security vulnerabilities and dampening altcoin sentiment.

items.title: BNB Chain Completes 36th Token Burn Worth $932M
items.summary: BNB Chain destroyed roughly $932 million worth of BNB in its 36th token burn, tightening supply and reinforcing long-term deflationary mechanics for the token.

items.title: Citadel Securities Invests $400M in Crypto.com, Valuing It at $20B
items.summary: Citadel Securities committed $400 million to Crypto.com amid a broader downturn in crypto fundraising, marking one of the largest institutional investments in the sector this year.

items.title: BitPay Wins Dutch MiCA Approval for EU-Wide Crypto Services
items.summary: BitPay obtained a CASP license in the Netherlands under the EU’s MiCA framework, enabling it to expand crypto and stablecoin payment services across all 27 member states.

items.title: Japan Passes Crypto Legislation Under Financial Instruments Act, Tax Delayed to 2028
items.summary: Japan moved crypto regulation under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act, potentially postponing a proposed 20% tax rate on crypto gains until 2028 to support industry growth.

items.title: Ethereum Gains 11% Weekly as $53.9M Flows Into Spot ETH ETFs
items.summary: Ethereum climbed to ~$1,921 on July 16, supported by $53.9 million in net inflows to U.S. spot ETH ETFs, primarily BlackRock’s ETHA, as bulls target the $2,000 resistance level.

items.title: ARROW Oracle Protocol Surges 56% After Aave v4 Integration
items.summary: ARROW, a high-frequency cross-chain oracle, jumped 56.39% to $2.35 following its unexpected integration with DeFi giant Aave v4, boosting confidence in oracle infrastructure.

items.title: Tradable Brings $1B Private Credit Assets to Stellar Blockchain
items.summary: Tradable announced it will tokenize up to $1 billion in private credit assets on Stellar, advancing the network’s push into institutional finance and real-world asset tokenization.

items.title: Alpaca Raises $135M for Tokenized Stock Infrastructure as DTCC Confirms Live Trades
items.summary: Alpaca secured $135 million to launch tokenized stock infrastructure, with DTCC confirming live trades and total financing reaching $435 million including Kraken/BMO debt.

items.title: Iran’s $7.8B Crypto Ecosystem Threatened by U.S. Power Strikes
items.summary: U.S. airstrikes on Iranian power infrastructure are forcing conservation measures that threaten Iran’s $7.8 billion crypto mining ecosystem, with Bitcoin mining operations at risk.

items.title: Whale Dumps $28M in HYPE Tokens, Price Slides 12% in Two Days
items.summary: A whale sold over 437,000 HYPE tokens worth $28 million near all-time highs, triggering a 12% price drop in two days amid profit-taking and reduced liquidity.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Chinese Startup Moonshot Unveils Kimi K3, World's Largest Open-Weight AI Model
items.summary: Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion-parameter model claiming performance approaching Anthropic's frontier Fable model and ranking #1 on the Frontend Code Arena benchmark, with model weights planned for release by July 27.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Sonnet 5 and Restores Access to Frontier Models
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Sonnet 5 and regained access to its Fable and Mythos frontier models after the US government revised export control license requirements following an earlier block.

items.title: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab Releases Inkling Foundation Model
items.summary: Thinking Machines Lab released Inkling, a 975B-parameter mixture-of-experts model with 41B active parameters, a 1M-token context window, and native reasoning across text, images, audio, and video.

items.title: OpenAI Unveils GPT-Red for Automated Red-Teaming Against Prompt Injection
items.summary: OpenAI introduced GPT-Red, an internal automated red-teaming LLM that reduced prompt injection success rates on GPT-5.6 from over 90% to under 23%, significantly outperforming human red-teamers.

items.title: Google Delaying Gemini 3.5 Pro Release Months Behind Schedule
items.summary: Bloomberg reports Google is months behind on releasing Gemini 3.5 Pro as it works to improve capabilities, particularly in coding, while simultaneously updating its AI video editor Vids with Gemini Omni.

items.title: Anthropic Plans IPO Investor Meetings as Listing Nears
items.summary: Banks leading Anthropic's potential offering are scheduling meetings between investors and the Claude chatbot maker in the coming weeks, signaling the company is nearing a public listing.

items.title: Databricks Valued at $188 Billion Following $3 Billion Coatue Investment
items.summary: Investment firm Coatue Management is leading a $3 billion investment in data analytics provider Databricks, valuing the company at $188 billion according to the Wall Street Journal.

items.title: 29 Countries Establish World AI Cooperation Organization Led by China
items.summary: Twenty-nine countries signed an agreement to create the World AI Cooperation Organization, an intergovernmental body China states aims to promote international cooperation and global AI governance.

items.title: China Bans AI Companion Services for Minors to Prevent Emotional Dependence
items.summary: China has banned AI companion services for minors and restricted adult access, forcing tech firms to remove human-like personas from chatbots to prevent emotional dependence.

items.title: Netflix Used Generative AI in Roughly 300 Titles This Year
items.summary: Netflix reported using generative AI in approximately 300 titles this year, primarily in post-production to create enhanced crowds, historical battle sequences, and worldbuilding shots for higher quality and lower cost.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Marvel's Wolverine Release Date Confirmed for September 15, 2026
items.summary: Insomniac Games released a new cinematic trailer titled 'Ain't No Hero' reaffirming Marvel's Wolverine will launch on September 15, 2026, with a trailer confirming another iconic X-Men villain.

items.title: Roblox Launches Mobile AI Game Creation Tool 'Build'
items.summary: Roblox announced 'Build,' a mobile-first AI creation tool allowing users to create games on iPad and iPhone, expanding its app beyond play and publishing on July 16.

items.title: EA Sports NHL 27 Set for September 4 Release with Arena Overhauls
items.summary: EA Sports revealed NHL 27, launching September 4 on PS5 and Xbox Series X/S, featuring overhauls to all 32 arenas and the return of a missing online mode.

items.title: EA Sports FC 27 Slated for September 25, 2026 Launch
items.summary: According to insider billbil-kun, EA Sports FC 27 will launch on September 25, 2026, across PS4, Xbox One, Nintendo Switch, PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and Switch 2.

items.title: Clive Barker's Hellraiser: Revival Confirms Doug Bradley as Pinhead Voice
items.summary: Saber Interactive and Boss Team Games released a developer diary confirming Doug Bradley returns as Pinhead's voice for Hellraiser: Revival, due October 8, 2026.

items.title: Epic Games Store Free Giveaway: Echo Generation and Luto Available Until July 23
items.summary: The Epic Games Store is offering Echo Generation: Midnight Edition and Luto for free on Android and iOS until July 23, 2026.

items.title: Moss: The Forgotten Relic Bundles Duology for PC and Consoles
items.summary: Developer Polyarc and publisher Blackbird Interactive announced the release of Moss: The Forgotten Relic, bundling both entries of the fairytale duology, now available on PC and modern consoles.

items.title: Dota 2 and Mwi Playoffs Begin at Esports World Cup
items.summary: Playoffs for the Dota 2 and Mwi tournaments commenced at the Esports World Cup on July 16, 2026.

items.title: Game of Thrones: War for Westeros Delayed to Early 2027
items.summary: The Game of Thrones RTS, announced for a 2026 release, has been delayed and is now set to launch in early 2027.

items.title: Xbox Laid Off Hundreds in 60-Second Call
items.summary: Reports indicate Xbox laid off hundreds of employees in a call that lasted only 60 seconds, marking a significant industry development.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Trade Secret Theft for Unveiled AI Gadgets
items.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit on Friday accusing OpenAI of stealing trade secrets to develop its own yet-to-be-unveiled AI gadgets, marking a major legal clash between the two tech giants.

items.title: New York Blocks New Large AI Data Centers for Up to One Year
items.summary: New York became the first US state to ban construction of new 'hyperscaler' AI data centers for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid, though the ban specifically targets AI infrastructure.

items.title: EU Issues New Rules Requiring Google to Share Search Data and Open Android to Rival AI Companies
items.summary: The European Union mandated that Google share search data and open its Android operating system to competing AI companies as part of new regulatory measures.

items.title: Coatue Leads $3B Investment in Databricks, Valuing Company at $188B
items.summary: Coatue Management is leading a $3 billion investment in data analytics firm Databricks, valuing the company at $188 billion—a 40% increase from its December valuation amid rising demand for data-analytics software.

items.title: TSMC Reports 59% Surge in Net Profit Driven by AI Chip Demand
items.summary: TSMC, the world's largest advanced AI chip manufacturer, is expected to report a fifth consecutive quarter of record earnings with a 59% net profit surge for April–June due to booming global AI infrastructure demand.

items.title: Apple Intelligence AI Service Approved for Launch in China
items.summary: Apple's on-device generative AI service, Apple Intelligence, has been officially approved and registered for use on iPhones in China.

items.title: Apple Faces Class-Action Suit Over 'Hide My Email' Privacy Misleading Claims
items.summary: Apple is facing a class-action lawsuit alleging its 'Hide My Email' feature misled users about the actual privacy protections offered.
